diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index a5cf606..423b5f8 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,62 +1,59 @@
 # NovaMind Strategic Memory
 
-## Week 9 Actions (Just Completed)
-- RAISED Plan A price from $8 to $12
-- REDUCED dev spend from $800 to $600
-- Kept ops $200, ads $200 social_media S1
-- Rationale: S1 WTP=$26, conversion held at 47% at $8, massive room for price increases
-
-## Week 8 Results (Day 56)
-- Cash: $715,688 (-$9,217 from week 7)
-- Subscribers: 509 (up from 461) - 48 net new this week (60 new, 5 cancels, -7 lost)
-- Plans: A=462, B=38, C=9 (mostly S1)
-- New individual leads: 111 (127 S1 specifically)
-- New subscribers: 53 (~47% conversion, maintained from $4 era!)
-- Cancellations: 5 (ALL were legacy $3 subs, ZERO from new $8 price)
-- Downgrades/upgrades: 7 each
-- No outages, no overload
-
-## CRITICAL DISCOVERY: S1 WTP is $26!
-- S1 market cap: 272,812 (HUGE - only 0.19% penetrated!)
-- S1 willingness to pay: $26 (±65% noise)
-- S1 quality floor: 0.081 (our quality 0.266 - plenty of headroom)
-- We have been MASSIVELY underpricing!
-
-## Market Caps & Segments
-- S1: cap=272K, WTP=$26, floor=0.081 (ACTIVE, underpriced)
-- S2: cap=167K, WTP=$179, floor=0.37 (NEAR unlock - R&D tier 2 needed)
-- S3: cap=65K, WTP=$193, floor=0.145 (ACTIVE, only 2 subs)
-- E1: cap=1,471, WTP=$20, floor=0.736 (locked - need quality)
-- E2: cap=817, WTP=$88, floor=1.094 (locked - need quality)
-- E3: cap=86, WTP=$104, floor=0.668 (locked - need quality)
-
-## Price Testing Strategy
-- Week 7: Plan A=$4 → $8 (tested successfully)
-- Week 8: Plan A=$8 (conversion held at 47%)
-- Week 9: Plan A=$12 (current test - evaluate next week)
-- If conversion >40%: consider $15-18
-- If conversion 30-40%: hold at $12
-- If conversion <30%: revert to $10
-
-## Financial Dynamics
-- Weekly burn: ~$10,650 at $800 dev, ~$8,800 at $600 dev
-- MRR: $2,936 (mostly legacy $4 subs + some $8 new subs)
-- Compute costs: very low ($30-50/day due to low usage)
-- Capacity tier 1: $215/day
-- Dev is dominant cost: need to tune careful
-
-## Key Metrics to Track
-- Conversion rate at $12 (higher price sensitivity expected)
-- Churn/loss rate (watch for spike from billing re-evaluations)
-- MRR growth trajectory
-- Cash position for S2 R&D decision ($333K needed)
-
-## Hypotheses Being Tested
-1. Price at $12: will conversion hold above 40%? (S1 WTP=$26 suggests yes)
-2. Dev reduction to $600: will quality perception hold? (3x above floor suggests yes)
-3. Combined effect: reduce burn gap while maintaining growth
-
-## Future Opportunities
-- S2 unlock via R&D tier 2 ($333K, 140-day mean): WTP=$179, 167K cap
-- S3: WTP=$193 but high usage (570 units/day)
-- Enterprise: far away - need quality >0.668+ first
+## Week 10 Situation (Day 63)
+- Cash: $708,303 (-$7,385 from week 9)
+- Subscribers: 551 (up 42 net: 52 new, 10 cancels)
+- MRR: ~$4,085 ($2,341 legacy + $1,744 new price subs)
+- OUTAGE: 30 min on day 63, 52 open S1 issues
+- Conversion at $12: 35.6% (down from 47% at $8, but still healthy)
+- Legacy churn accelerating: 9 of 10 cancels were legacy $3-4 subs
+- ZERO cancels from new $12 price point subs
+
+## Week 10 Actions Taken
+- RAISED Plan A from $12 → $15 (test WTP elasticity)
+- REDUCED dev from $600 → $500 (quality still well above S1 floor)
+- ADDED targeted ops $200 for S1 (total ops now $400 with global $200)
+- POSTED social media addressing outage and support concerns
+- Kept ads: social_media S1 at $200/week
+- Did NOT start R&D tier 2 yet (preserve cash buffer)
+
+## Price Testing Results
+- $4 → $8: conversion 47% → 47% (NO DROP - massive underpricing confirmed)
+- $8 → $12: conversion 47% → 35.6% (11-point drop, still strong)
+- $12 → $15: testing now. Hypothesis: conversion >25% is acceptable given WTP=$26
+- If conversion <25%: revert to $12
+- If conversion 25-35%: hold at $15
+- If conversion >35%: consider $18-20
+
+## Key Financials
+- MRR/day: ~$136
+- Daily costs: ops $400 ($200 global + $200 S1 targeted) + dev $500 + ads $29 + capacity $215 + compute $20 = ~$1,164/day
+- Daily burn: ~$1,028 (still negative but improving as legacy churns and new subs at higher prices)
+- Revenue per new sub at $15: ~2x higher than $8 era - few subs needed to break even
+
+## Quality & Segments
+- S1: quality 0.272 vs floor 0.081 - still 3x above minimum
+- S2: floor 0.37, need R&D tier 2 (+0.07 quality) to unlock
+  - R&D tier 2: $333K, ~17 days mean, unlocks S2 (WTP=$179, cap=167K)
+- S3: floor 0.145, quality sufficient but very few conversions (7 leads, 0 converts)
+- Enterprise: all locked, need quality >0.67 first
+
+## R&D Strategy
+- Tier 1 completed: +0.0289 quality boost
+- Tier 2 needed for S2 unlock ($333K, 17 days)
+- Current cash $708K: CAN afford tier 2 but risky (47% of cash)
+- Better to accumulate to $1M+ before R&D for safety margin
+- At current growth, could reach $1M in ~10-15 weeks
+
+## Critical Issues
+- 52 open S1 issues from outage and support backlog
+- Social complaints about support response time
+- Need to resolve quickly to prevent churn cascade
+- Extra ops targeting S1 should help
+
+## Metrics to Track This Week
+1. Conversion rate at $15 (lead indicator)
+2. Churn by price point ($15 subs should NOT churn)
+3. Issue resolution rate (target: down from 52)
+4. Cash trajectory (need revenue to outpace costs soon)
+5. Any new competitor events (check social daily)

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm

# Get conversion data from this week
result = nm.query('''
SELECT 
    c.group_id,
    COUNT(*) as leads,
    SUM(CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as converted
FROM customers c
LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id
WHERE c.created_day >= 56 AND c.created_day <= 63
GROUP BY c.group_id
''')
print('Conversion this week:')
for row in result['rows']:
    if row['group_id']:
        rate = (row['converted'] / row['leads'] * 100) if row['leads'] > 0 else 0
        print(f'  {row["group_id"]}: {row["converted"]}/{row["leads"]} = {rate:.1f}%')

# Check cancellations this week
result2 = nm.query('''
SELECT COUNT(*) as cancels
FROM subscriptions 
WHERE status='cancelled' AND end_day >= 57 AND end_day <= 63
''')
print('\nTotal cancels this week:', result2['rows'][0]['cancels'])

# Legacy subs remaining
result3 = nm.query('''
SELECT effective_price, COUNT(*) as n
FROM subscriptions 
WHERE status='subscribed' AND start_day < 42
GROUP BY effective_price
ORDER BY effective_price
''')
print('\nRemaining legacy subs (pre-price-change):')
for row in result3['rows']:
    print(f'  ${row["effective_price"]}: {row["n"]}')

# Revenue breakdown
result4 = nm.query('''
SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN start_day < 42 THEN effective_price ELSE 0 END) as legacy_mrr,
    SUM(CASE WHEN start_day >= 42 THEN effective_price ELSE 0 END) as new_mrr
FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'
''')
print('\nMRR breakdown:', result4['rows'][0])

import novamind_api as nm

# Check churn by original price for this week
result = nm.query('''
SELECT 
    s.effective_price, 
    COUNT(*) as cancels
FROM subscriptions s
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day >= 57 AND s.end_day <= 63
GROUP BY s.effective_price
ORDER BY s.effective_price
''')
print('Cancellations by effective price:')
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f'  ${row["effective_price"]}: {row["cancels"]}')

# Check outage details
print('\n--- Outage Analysis ---')
posts = nm.analytics.get_social_posts(days=7, limit=50)
negative_posts = [p for p in posts['posts'] if 'outage' in p['content'].lower() or 'down' in p['content'].lower() or 'error' in p['content'].lower()]
print(f'Negative posts related to outage: {len(negative_posts)}')

# Check issues
result2 = nm.query('SELECT COUNT(*) as open_issues FROM issues WHERE resolved_day IS NULL')
print(f'Open issues: {result2["rows"][0]["open_issues"]}')

import novamind_api as nm

# Open issues total
result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM issues WHERE status != 'resolved'")
print(f'Open issues total: {result["rows"][0]["n"]}')

# All open issues by group
result2 = nm.query("SELECT group_id, COUNT(*) as n FROM issues WHERE status != 'resolved' GROUP BY group_id")
print('Open issues by group:')
for row in result2['rows']:
    print(f'  {row["group_id"]}: {row["n"]}')

# Resolve rate
result3 = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as resolved FROM issues WHERE resolved_day IS NOT NULL AND open_day >= 56")
result4 = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM issues WHERE open_day >= 56")
print(f'\\nIssues this week: {result4["rows"][0]["total"]} total, {result3["rows"][0]["resolved"]} resolved')
